Regulator_Type.reason: Dir2a is one of the candidate genes tested among dirigent family members for coumestrol biosynthesis, and overexpression increased coumestrol; the paper treats it as a coumestrol-biosynthesis-related candidate/regulator.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states PTS1 belongs to the dirigent protein family and that 52 dirigent family members were identified; Dir2a is among the tested candidate dirigent genes.

Source_Species.reason: The study concerns soybean genes and experiments were conducted in soybean.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Soybean is explicitly Glycine max in the paper.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The candidate genes were functionally tested in Williams 82 hairy roots, and Dir2a was later cloned from Williams 82 and RPS1D; Williams 82 is an appropriate source variety stated in Results.

Applied_Species.reason: All gene overexpression assays were performed in soybean hairy roots.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is soybean, Glycine max.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Dir2a was overexpressed in Williams 82 hairy roots and also in susceptible cultivar P20.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper directly links Dir2a overexpression with increased coumestrol (CMS) accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Coumestrol is identified as a coumestan/benzofuran-type metabolite and described as a phytoalexin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper states coumestrol was highly enriched in roots, nodules, and mature leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show Dir2a overexpression significantly elevated CMS in Williams 82 hairy roots and in P20 increased CMS with enhanced resistance to P. sojae.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are present in Results: shortlisted by correlation plus inducible transcript analyses, increased CMS in Williams 82 OE hairy roots, and about 70% reduced infection in P20 OE roots.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: The paper supports only that Dir2a affects the coumestrol biosynthesis module/pathway; it explicitly does not establish exact direct downstream targets.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Dir2a overexpression increased coumestrol accumulation and strengthened resistance, supporting a positive influence on the target product.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: A positive effect on coumestrol biosynthesis/accumulation is supported, and the absence of identified direct transcriptional targets is consistent with the paper.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The Results describe candidate intersection analysis, transient hairy-root overexpression, qRT-PCR validation, coumestrol quantification, and P. sojae infection/biomass assays.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Positive regulation of coumestrol accumulation is supported; the paper explicitly says the exact biochemical mechanism remains unresolved.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The experiments involve P. sojae infection and hairy-root transient overexpression.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Coumestrol is shown to suppress P. sojae infection by inhibiting cyst germination and also to inhibit spore germination across several pathogens.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states candidate genes were selected by correlation between transcript abundance and coumestrol across tissues and by P. sojae-inducible expression analysis.

Omics_Data.reason: PRJNA210431 is explicitly cited for RNA-seq, and metabolomics across cultivars/tissues was used in candidate screening.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Dir2a is presented as involved in/regulating coumestrol biosynthesis.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: P. sojae infection is the inducive condition used to identify candidate genes and assess CMS accumulation.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly state in vitro one-pot assays were inconclusive and that deduced amino acid sequences were identical between Williams 82 and RPS1D.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper supports pathogen-responsive candidate behavior and root-focused assays, but does not explicitly state a detailed expression pattern for Dir2a specifically in the applied varieties beyond candidate screening/OE contexts.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper states these candidates have strong potential for application in improving disease resistance by boosting CMS accumulation, including in susceptible cultivars.
